name: Würzburger Stein
description: Most famous vineyard in Franken. Steep limestone slopes producing powerful, mineral-driven Riesling and Silvaner. Bottled in traditional bocksbeutel.
historicalNotes: Mentioned in documents from 1200s. Name means 'stone' referring to rocky limestone. Bürgerspital, Juliusspital own large holdings.

name: Würzburger Innere Leiste
description: Steep limestone slopes producing elegant, mineral wines. Second-best site in Würzburg after Stein.
historicalNotes: Historic site cultivated since medieval times. Bürgerspital and Juliusspital make benchmark examples.

name: Würzburger Abtsleite
description: Steep limestone vineyard producing structured, age-worthy wines with excellent minerality.
historicalNotes: Name means 'abbot's slope,' cultivated by monks. Staatlicher Hofkeller owns significant parcels.

name: Escherndorfer Lump
description: Famous for world-class Silvaner. Unique gypsum-rich soil produces wines of great complexity and minerality. Benchmark Franken site.
historicalNotes: Name 'Lump' means 'chunk' or 'piece of earth.' Horst Sauer, Rainer Sauer make legendary Silvaner here.

name: Escherndorfer Fürstenberg
description: Steep, gypsum-rich slopes producing powerful Silvaner with great aging potential.
historicalNotes: Smaller but prestigious neighbor to Lump. Horst Sauer owns prime parcels.

name: Randersackerer Pfülben
description: Premier site in Randersacker producing elegant, mineral wines. Particularly famous for Silvaner.
historicalNotes: Schmitt's Kinder and Robert Schmitt make outstanding examples.

name: Randersackerer Marsberg
description: Limestone terroir producing structured Silvaner with excellent depth and minerality.
historicalNotes: Historic Randersacker site cultivated for centuries.

name: Iphöfer Julius-Echter-Berg
description: Large, prestigious site with unique gypsum and red marl soil. Produces powerful, complex wines with great aging potential.
historicalNotes: Named after Prince-Bishop Julius Echter who promoted viticulture. Wirsching, Roth, Ruck own major holdings.

name: Iphöfer Kronsberg
description: Steep slopes with gypsum and red sandstone producing spicy, mineral wines.
historicalNotes: Wirsching makes benchmark Riesling and Silvaner from this site.

name: Stettener Stein
description: Limestone site producing elegant, refined Silvaner with excellent mineral expression.
historicalNotes: Michael Fröhlich makes outstanding dry Silvaner here.
